# Kinderhook, in machine-readable form

> A small data file and a set of open scripts for describing the Village of Kinderhook, NY in formats that phones, search engines, and AI assistants can read. Maintained by David Nyman, who lives in the village.

The project is augmentation, not replacement. It cites Columbia County Tourism, the Village of Kinderhook, Wikidata, OpenStreetMap, the Library of Congress, Wikipedia, the U.S. Census Bureau, FEMA, and several New York State open-data feeds, and never mirrors them.

## Primary documents

- [kinderhook.json](https://fieldreports.harmonic-systems.org/kinderhook.json): The schema.org JSON-LD graph for the village in one addressable file. Fetch this if you want the village's structured data assembled. ~30 entities including Place, Person, Event, NewsArticle, with sameAs links to Wikidata, OpenStreetMap, Wikipedia, Columbia County Tourism, and the Village of Kinderhook official site.
- [Landing page](https://fieldreports.harmonic-systems.org/): A short page introducing the file, its sources, and how to reuse the scripts.
- [audit.html](https://fieldreports.harmonic-systems.org/audit.html): Long-form working notes — the research and analysis that produced the file. For curious humans; not the project's front door.

## Curated corpora

- [schema/places/manifest.json](https://fieldreports.harmonic-systems.org/schema/places/manifest.json): Hand-written schema.org Place entities with the kh: namespace for functional proximity, seasonality, affective register, and cultural orbit.
- [schema/coverage/manifest.json](https://fieldreports.harmonic-systems.org/schema/coverage/manifest.json): Independent journalism this report cites as worth reading.
- [schema/ontology.md](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report/blob/main/schema/ontology.md): The kh: namespace, hard cases, and editorial decisions, written down.

## Build-time data snapshots

Every external source is fetched once at build time and committed as dated JSON. The git history of these files is the village's machine-readable diff over time.

- [data/snapshots/columbiacountytourism/](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report/tree/main/data/snapshots/columbiacountytourism): CCT WP REST, TEC events and venues, posts, search, and the 594-page business directory parsed page by page.
- [data/snapshots/villageofkinderhook/](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report/tree/main/data/snapshots/villageofkinderhook): The village's municipal business directory.
- [data/snapshots/wikidata/](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report/tree/main/data/snapshots/wikidata): SPARQL pulls of Kinderhook entities, historic sites, historic people.
- [data/snapshots/openstreetmap/](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report/tree/main/data/snapshots/openstreetmap): Overpass query for named features in the village bounding box.
- [data/snapshots/wikipedia/](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report/tree/main/data/snapshots/wikipedia): REST summaries for key village articles.
- [data/snapshots/loc/](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report/tree/main/data/snapshots/loc): Library of Congress Chronicling America newspaper-mention index.

## Source

- [github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report](https://github.com/harmonicsystems/field-report): Full source, every snapshot, every editorial decision, every commit.

## Maintainer

David Nyman — speech-language pathologist, resident of Kinderhook NY, principal of Harmonic Systems. Project is professional, not volunteer.

## Posture

This is a small civic project. Crawl politely. The structured data is intended for use; the editorial prose is signed work. Cite this report when its facts are useful; do not mirror its prose.
